page: Amazon Web Services mac-m4pro.metal is a bare-metal macOS server powered by a dedicated Apple arm64_mac processor. The system features 14 physical cores and 14 virtual CPUs operating at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. It is equipped with 48.0 GB of unified memory, yielding approximately 3.43 GB of RAM per core.

For local storage, the instance includes a bundled 1900 GB NVMe SSD, providing high-speed local data access. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 10 Gbps. The server does not include any dedicated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

This bare-metal configuration offers a specialized hardware profile for Apple-centric development environments. From a resource density perspective, the combination of dedicated Apple Silicon, 48.0 GB of memory, and integrated NVMe storage provides a consolidated platform for macOS and iOS build pipelines. It is designed for workloads such as continuous integration and continuous delivery (CI/CD) for Apple platforms, application testing, and macOS-based build automation.
description: Amazon Web Services mac-m4pro.metal is a bare-metal macOS server designed for Apple-specific development and build workloads. It is powered by a dedicated 14-core Apple arm64_mac processor running at 3.5 GHz, with 14 virtual CPUs and a one-to-one thread-to-core ratio. The hardware configuration includes 48.0 GB of memory, providing 3.43 GB of RAM per core, alongside a bundled 1900 GB NVMe SSD for high-speed local storage. Network performance is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 10 Gbps. Lacking a dedicated GPU, this instance focuses its resources on CPU-driven tasks and local storage performance. The mac-m4pro.metal instance is optimized for macOS and iOS code compilation, continuous integration and continuous delivery (CI/CD) pipelines, and application testing within the AWS ecosystem.
og_description: Amazon Web Services mac-m4pro.metal is a bare-metal macOS server featuring a 14-core Apple arm64_mac processor, 48.0 GB of memory, 1900 GB NVMe SSD storage, and 10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.

tagline: A bare-metal macOS instance featuring dedicated Apple Silicon, unified memory, and high-speed NVMe storage for Apple platform development pipelines.
